# Review: deepmind/57.lean

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports` at line 17.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line (line 19).
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# ErdŇĎs Problem 57`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to the source: `[erdosproblems.com/57](https://www.erdosproblems.com/57)`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem (infinite chromatic number implies divergence of reciprocals of odd cycle lengths).
- [x] Bibliography entries present: `[ErHa66]` citing ErdŇĎs and Hajnal (1966), `[LiMo20]` citing Liu and Montgomery (2020).

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos57 ... end Erdos57`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase`: `Erdos57`.
- [x] `open SimpleGraph Finset` appears **before** the namespace block (line 33, before line 35).

## 5. Custom Definitions

- N/A: No custom `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations in this file.

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_57` (snake_case, matching namespace number).
- [x] Name follows Lean/Mathlib `snake_case` conventions.
- [x] Theorem has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research solved, AMS 5]`.
- [x] Main theorem has a docstring (lines 37‚Äď45) referencing `[ErHa66]` and `[LiMo20]`.

### `answer()` usage

The problem is stated as a direct assertion: "If G is a graph with infinite chromatic number and a‚āĀ < a‚āā < ‚čĮ are the lengths of its odd cycles, then ‚ąĎ 1/aŠĶĘ = ‚ąě." This is **Case 3** (direct assertion), so no `answer()` wrapper is needed.

- [x] Theorem type is `P` directly ‚ÄĒ no `answer()` wrapper. Correct for Case 3.
- N/A: Problem was proved affirmatively by Liu and Montgomery, so no negation needed.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Every theorem has **exactly one** `@[category ...]`.
- [x] Value is valid: `research solved` (conjecture of ErdŇĎs‚ÄďHajnal proved by Liu‚ÄďMontgomery, but not formally verified in Lean).
- [x] Category is appropriate: `research solved` with `sorry` in proof body is correct (the result is known but not yet formally proved).

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Every theorem has **exactly one** `@[AMS ...]` attribute.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed: `5`.
- [x] Numbers are in **ascending numeric order**: only one number, trivially satisfied.
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] `5` is a valid AMS MSC2020 code (Combinatorics).

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Theorem uses `by` + `sorry` (multi-line form: `:= by\n  sorry`), which is acceptable.
- [x] No `sorry` in definitions (no definitions present).
- N/A: Not an open problem, so no concern about informal proof sketches.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- N/A: No variants in this file. The problem is a single assertion with no sub-parts.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout (continuation lines in the theorem signature use 4-space indent, standard Lean style).
- [x] Unicode math symbols used: `‚ąÄ`, `‚ąÉ`, `‚ąą`, `‚ąß`, `‚Č§`, `‚ąĎ`, `‚Ąē`, `‚ĄĚ`, `‚ä§`.
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all under 100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` not run as part of this review ‚ÄĒ requires manual verification.
- [ ] Custom linter passes not verified ‚ÄĒ requires build.

## Summary

**Result: PASS** (pending build verification)

The file is clean and fully conformant with the checklist. The theorem correctly formalizes the ErdŇĎs‚ÄďHajnal conjecture (proved by Liu‚ÄďMontgomery) that a graph with infinite chromatic number must have divergent reciprocal sum of odd cycle lengths. The formalization captures "‚ąĎ 1/aŠĶĘ = ‚ąě" as the standard unboundedness condition (for every B, a finite subset of odd cycle lengths has reciprocal sum ‚Č• B), which is mathematically correct. The use of `Finset ‚Ąē` ensures the cycle lengths in each witness set are distinct. Properly categorized as `research solved`, well-documented with paper references, and no issues found.

    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 57
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/57](https://www.erdosproblems.com/57)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíIf $G$ is a graph with infinite chromatic number and $a_1 < a_2 < \cdots$ are the lengths
    25‚Üíof its odd cycles, then $\sum 1/a_i = \infty$. Conjectured by ErdŇĎs and Hajnal [ErHa66],
    26‚Üíproved by Liu and Montgomery [LiMo20].
    27‚Üí
    28‚Üí[ErHa66] ErdŇĎs, P. and Hajnal, A., _On chromatic number of graphs and set-systems_, 1966.
    29‚Üí
    30‚Üí[LiMo20] Liu, H. and Montgomery, R., _A solution to ErdŇĎs and Hajnal's odd cycle problem_, 2020.
    31‚Üí-/
    32‚Üí

    34‚Üí
    35‚Üínamespace Erdos57

    37‚Üí/--
    38‚ÜíErdŇĎs Problem 57 (Conjectured by ErdŇĎs-Hajnal [ErHa66], proved by Liu-Montgomery [LiMo20]):
    39‚ÜíIf $G$ is a graph with infinite chromatic number and $a_1 < a_2 < \cdots$ are the lengths of
    40‚Üíthe odd cycles of $G$, then $\sum 1/a_i = \infty$.
    41‚Üí
    42‚ÜíWe formalize "$\sum 1/a_i = \infty$" as: for any real bound $B$, there exists a finite set $T$
    43‚Üíof odd natural numbers, each of which is the length of some cycle in $G$, whose reciprocals
    44‚Üísum to at least $B$.
    45‚Üí-/
    46‚Üí@[category research solved, AMS 5]
    47‚Üítheorem erdos_57 {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V)
    48‚Üí    (hŌá : G.chromaticNumber = ‚ä§) :
    49‚Üí    ‚ąÄ (B : ‚ĄĚ), ‚ąÉ (T : Finset ‚Ąē),
    50‚Üí      (‚ąÄ n ‚ąą T, Odd n ‚ąß ‚ąÉ v : V, ‚ąÉ p : G.Walk v v, p.IsCycle ‚ąß p.length = n) ‚ąß
    51‚Üí      B ‚Č§ ‚ąĎ n ‚ąą T, (1 / (n : ‚ĄĚ)) := by
    52‚Üí  sorry
    53‚Üí
    54‚Üíend Erdos57
    55‚Üí

file.content:
/-
Copyright 2026 The Formal Conjectures Authors.

Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
You may obtain a copy of the License at

    https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.
-/

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# ErdŇĎs Problem 57

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/57](https://www.erdosproblems.com/57)

If $G$ is a graph with infinite chromatic number and $a_1 < a_2 < \cdots$ are the lengths
of its odd cycles, then $\sum 1/a_i = \infty$. Conjectured by ErdŇĎs and Hajnal [ErHa66],
proved by Liu and Montgomery [LiMo20].

[ErHa66] ErdŇĎs, P. and Hajnal, A., _On chromatic number of graphs and set-systems_, 1966.

[LiMo20] Liu, H. and Montgomery, R., _A solution to ErdŇĎs and Hajnal's odd cycle problem_, 2020.
-/

open SimpleGraph Finset

namespace Erdos57

/--
ErdŇĎs Problem 57 (Conjectured by ErdŇĎs-Hajnal [ErHa66], proved by Liu-Montgomery [LiMo20]):
If $G$ is a graph with infinite chromatic number and $a_1 < a_2 < \cdots$ are the lengths of
the odd cycles of $G$, then $\sum 1/a_i = \infty$.

We formalize "$\sum 1/a_i = \infty$" as: for any real bound $B$, there exists a finite set $T$
of odd natural numbers, each of which is the length of some cycle in $G$, whose reciprocals
sum to at least $B$.
-/
@[category research solved, AMS 5]
theorem erdos_57 {V : Type*} (G : SimpleGraph V)
    (hŌá : G.chromaticNumber = ‚ä§) :
    ‚ąÄ (B : ‚ĄĚ), ‚ąÉ (T : Finset ‚Ąē),
      (‚ąÄ n ‚ąą T, Odd n ‚ąß ‚ąÉ v : V, ‚ąÉ p : G.Walk v v, p.IsCycle ‚ąß p.length = n) ‚ąß
      B ‚Č§ ‚ąĎ n ‚ąą T, (1 / (n : ‚ĄĚ)) := by
  sorry

end Erdos57

content.text: The review result is **PASS**. No changes needed.
